How many of you have a relationship that's five years or longer? Raise your hands. Now, everybody look around at them because you don't know anything. Look at me. Howard, make a note of anybody who doesn't have their hand up. Will you? All right, 25. 50. 100. OK, all right. Now, what's it a demonstration of? You can put them down now. It's a good idea because it's a great demonstration of value, particularly in a business like ours because the only reason you're spending money, generally speaking, is because what you're buying, you're using to make money. And so you come back and get some more. In some other businesses, it might be a little different. So there's easy, quick, simple, dramatic demonstration. Again, since all of the super high earners have it. Now, we have a, I'm going to butcher his name. It's not Kapoozie, but it's close to it. Kapoozie, the home improvement guy. Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah. So he's got a DVD he's put together about himself and the thing he wants to sell. And it's a business thing. But one of the interesting things about him, arguably, the only interesting thing about him. Well, I mean, on the surface, how many of you are fascinated with a sales superstar from the home improvement business? I mean, it's more interesting than like being an accountant or a lawyer or something. But I mean, it's not exactly, oh, great. I'm going to spend two hours at a dinner table with this guy. But he's a race car driver. Well, now that's interesting. So where's he got the footage of him driving race cars? At the end of the DVD. No, no, no, no, no, no, no. It should be at the beginning of the DVD. Why? Because it's dramatic demonstration of two things. One, Courage Guts, all the stuff he wants to sell and is how to be successful program. More importantly, it's dramatic demonstration this is an interesting guy. This guy's like cool. This guy's somebody I want to be around. I want to know more about. Now, is it rationally connected? No, not really necessarily. But it's the best thing he's got by far. And it ought to be used, not left as an oh, by the way. So you want to think about the whole list of status producing things.
